quotes: Central nervous system cells retained the original fine-grained annotations from Braun et al., as these annotations were informed by spatial context
The roof-plate independent Class B dI6 commissural inhibitory interneurons express Lbx1, Lhx1, Lhx5, and are Pax2 positive, indicating a GABAergic fate
These cells may also use glycine for neurotransmission
Dmrt3, a novel marker in dl6 interneuron was traced to play a key role in locomotor circuitry and in development of commissural interneurons
The dorsal-most interneuron class, dI1, is characterized by expression of Lhx2 and Lhx9.
Lbx1 is essential for the specification and migration of the Class B neurons dI4-6 in the E12.5 neural tube
dI4/6 are inhibitory GABAergic neuronal types, utilizing GABA as a neurotransmitter, whilst Lmx1b-expressing dI5 is glutamatergic, utilizing VGLUT2 as a neurotransmitter
Dl1 neurons are generated from a pool of progenitor cells expressing Math1, and can be identified by two LIM transcription factors, Lhx2 and Lhx9.

The Lim-HD proteins Lhx9 and Lhx1 serve as a binary switch in controlling the rostral versus caudal longitudinal turning of the caudal commissural axons. Lhx1 determines caudal turning and Lhx9 triggers rostral turning.
Tlx1 and Tlx3, that are required for the specification of excitatory (glutamatergic) fates and these are only expressed in dorsal dI3, dI5 and DIL B cells
The dorsal part contains six dorsal progenitor (dP) domains (dP1-dP6) and then differentiate into the dorsal interneuron (dI) populations (dI1-dI6).

the dP1 and dP3-5 domains express Atoh1 (arrowheads, B) and Ascl1 (arrowheads, C), respectively, in the E11.5 mouse embryo. The dP1 cells give rise to Lhx2 + dI1s while Isl1 + dI3s emerge from the dP3-5 population.
